note: RQ3 parameter sensitivity, real-bytecode. T from existing 240-run data; beta from rq3_beta sweeps (3 vals x12x5); k pending (k-sweep running).

T_sensitivity.finding: DR saturates fast; all 12 bridges by T=2s, 100% of runs by 30s; knee ~1-5s; 60s budget ample

beta_sensitivity.finding: Negligible sensitivity: DR/TTE/XCC flat across beta in {0.1,0.4,0.7}; default 0.4 fine

k_sensitivity.metric: DR/TTE/XCC vs RAG top_k (ATG fixed, Module 2 regenerated)
k_sensitivity.finding: Negligible sensitivity: DR/TTE/XCC flat across k in {1,3,5,10}; k=3 default fine. Reconstructed bugs reachable with as little as 1 retrieved exploit.

overall_finding: On the reconstructed benchmark BridgeSentry is robust to both RAG k and waypoint beta (flat DR/TTE/XCC); the only parameter that materially moves detection is the time budget T, which needs ~2s to reach 12/12 bridges. Consistent with RQ1/RQ2 DR-saturation.
